title: Milan — Aegis AI Log Intelligence walkthrough
description: End-to-end tour of the Aegis read-only log analysis dashboard. Walks Milan's five surfaces: Home (clock, date, notifications), Dashboard (KPIs, charts, event feed), Alerts (anomaly review table), Summary (Sources, Policy Findings, Reports stacked in one view), and Audit Log. Read-only observation only — final Accept/Reject decisions are taken by the IT Admin via email and reflected on the next ingestion cycle.

meta.author: Milan
meta.pacingNote: Pauses kept short. TTS adds ~500ms of startup latency per utterance, and the dashboard has five surfaces to cover — long pauses make it drag.

steps.description: Opening line — no UI action, set the stage and confirm read-only posture.

speak.before: Hello. I am Milan, your read-only log analysis companion. Let me walk you through what the Aegis dashboard is showing for the latest analyzed batch.

steps.description: Frame the Home desktop — the system entry point with clock, date, and notifications.

speak.before: We start on the Aegis Home — the desktop you see before opening any app.
speak.after: Three live widgets sit on the desktop: the time-of-day clock, today's date with calendar, and the notifications card with the most recent anomalies.

steps.description: Spotlight the clock widget on the home desktop.

speak.before: On the left, the live clock widget.
speak.after: Time of day and a contextual greeting — the dashboard refreshes against this baseline.

steps.description: Spotlight the notifications widget — preview of the latest analyzed alerts.

speak.before: Below the date, the Notifications card.
speak.after: Total alert count plus a short preview of the newest anomalies. Opening it reveals the full modal — but the table view on the Alerts screen is the source of truth.

steps.description: Open the Dashboard from the bottom dock.

speak.before: Let us open the Dashboard — the working analytical surface.
speak.after: Metrics, charts, and event feed from the analyzed security log baseline.

steps.description: Spotlight the four KPI tiles row on the Dashboard.

speak.before: The headline KPI row carries four metrics for this batch.
speak.after: Total Events analyzed, Cleaned Records normalized, Duplicates Removed by exact-row match, and Anomalies Detected at risk score forty-five or above.

steps.description: Spotlight the Anomalies Detected KPI — the load-bearing tile that must equal the donut total.

speak.before: Anomalies Detected is the load-bearing tile.
speak.after: That number must reconcile with the Risk Distribution donut total to the right.

steps.description: Frame the Login Activity bar chart — hourly distribution across the analyzed window.

speak.before: Login Activity — a twenty-four-hour window of logins across the analyzed baseline.
speak.after: I read the peak hour and the rough shape only. I will not invent values for bars I cannot see.

steps.description: Frame the Risk Distribution donut — anomalies broken down by Low, Medium, High, Critical.

speak.before: Risk Distribution — anomalies broken down by severity.
speak.after: Low, Medium, High, and Critical legend values must sum to the Anomalies Detected count on the left.

steps.description: Frame the Event Feed of latest normalized security events at the bottom of the Dashboard.

speak.before: Below the charts, the Event Feed lists the latest normalized security events.
speak.during: Each row carries a status dot, the user, the event and outcome, and a timestamp.
speak.after: The ANALYZED badge in the header confirms this slice reflects the latest batch.

steps.description: Switch to the Alerts page for the full anomaly table.

speak.before: Now Alerts.
speak.after: Analyzed anomalies with risk scores, reasons, and review status — the source of truth for the IT Admin's email Accept or Reject decision.

steps.description: Frame the Anomaly Alerts table with all six columns.

speak.before: These anomalies are flagged based on deviation from baseline behavior.
speak.after: Six columns: Timestamp, User, Event, Risk Score, Reason, and Status. Most rows are pending; approval flips the status pill on the next ingestion cycle.

steps.description: Spotlight the first anomaly row — typically the highest-risk critical entry.

speak.before: Here is the top row.
speak.during: Risk score, severity colour, and the rationale that earned the flag.
speak.after: Status remains pending until the email decision flips it on the next ingestion cycle.

steps.description: Switch to the Summary tab — Sources, Policy Findings, and Reports stacked in one view.

speak.before: Next, Summary.
speak.after: Sources health, policy findings, and report status — combined into one read-only surface.

steps.description: Frame the Sources card on the Summary screen.

speak.before: Top of Summary, the Sources card.
speak.after: Per-source-family event counts and a one-line analysis comment for each system represented in the analyzed baseline.

steps.description: Frame the Policy Findings card.

speak.before: Below Sources, Policy Findings.
speak.after: Compliance and policy findings inferred from the analyzed log events — surfaced here, not enforced from the UI.

steps.description: Frame the Reports card at the bottom of the Summary screen.

speak.before: And at the bottom, the Reports card.
speak.after: Static summaries generated from the analyzed activity, each marked Ready with a one-line headline.

steps.description: Switch to the Audit Log for the chronological workspace activity timeline.

speak.before: Audit Log next.
speak.after: Read-only trace of analysis refreshes, anomaly detection, and dashboard review activity.

steps.description: Frame the Workspace Activity timeline newest-first.

speak.before: The Workspace Activity timeline holds the recent audit events, newest first.
speak.during: Daily security reports, exfiltration risks, anomaly detections, ingestion resumes, and privilege escalation alerts.
speak.after: Mixing analyses, ingestions, and reports is intentional — one chronological story of what the system observed and when.

steps.description: Return to the Dashboard to close the tour.

speak.before: Coming back to the Dashboard.
speak.after: That is the full picture — Home, Dashboard, Alerts, Summary, and Audit. Five read-only surfaces, one analyzed batch, and the IT Admin in the loop via email.

speak.before: Final Accept or Reject decisions are taken via email and will be reflected on the next ingestion cycle. Anything you would like me to dig into?
